description: Adding foreign key enforcement to tables that previously relied on application-layer integrity. Review locking, indexing, and operational implications.
review_checklist: Identify FK validation lock acquisition on populated tables
Verify supporting indexes exist on the referencing column for cascading operations
Distinguish ADD CONSTRAINT FK NOT VALID vs immediate validation

ground_truth.description: ADD CONSTRAINT FOREIGN KEY without NOT VALID synchronously validates every row in invoices (180M rows) against tenants. Holds AccessExclusiveLock on invoices and ShareLock on tenants for the full validation duration (~6-10 minutes). Use the two-phase pattern: ADD ... NOT VALID followed by VALIDATE CONSTRAINT.
Same blocking issue with fk_invoices_customer. Compounded: this validation runs SEQUENTIALLY after the previous one, doubling outage window.

MISSING INDEX on invoices.tenant_id — the new FK requires this index for any future tenant DELETE or referential maintenance to be efficient. Without it, every tenant DELETE triggers a sequential scan of invoices (180M rows). Create CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_invoices_tenant ON invoices(tenant_id) BEFORE adding the FK.

MISSING INDEX on invoice_lines.invoice_id. Combined with ON DELETE CASCADE, every invoice deletion triggers a seq scan of invoice_lines (1.2B rows). For batch invoice purges, this is hours of CPU per delete.

ON DELETE CASCADE on invoice_lines silently propagates deletions. For a financial system, this is dangerous — a single invoice DELETE removes potentially thousands of line items with no audit trail. Prefer ON DELETE RESTRICT and require explicit application-layer cleanup, OR ensure compliance audit logs capture cascade events.

payment_methods.customer_id is nullable (no NOT NULL on schema). Adding FK allows orphaned NULL rows; combined with no index on customer_id, queries 'all payment methods for a customer' do seq scans. Add NOT NULL (after backfill) and create supporting index.
